text: A car is initially at rest on a straight horizontal road. The car then accelerates with constant acceleration of 3.2 m/s. Find the speed of the car after 5 s.
improve: Practice constant acceleration formulae (suvat). Remember v = u + at.
subject: Mech Y1

text: A car initially at rest accelerates at 3.2 m/s for 5 s. Find the distance travelled in the first 5 s.
improve: Practice s = ut + ½at². When starting from rest, u = 0.
subject: Mech Y1

text: A particle of mass 5 kg on a rough horizontal plane is pulled by a horizontal force of 28 N. Find the normal reaction.
explain: R = mg = 5 × 9.8 = 49 N (no vertical acceleration)
improve: On a horizontal plane, R = mg. Always resolve vertically first.
subject: Mech Y1

text: A 5 kg particle on a horizontal plane is pulled by 28 N and accelerates at 1.4 m/s. Find the frictional force F.
improve: Use F = ma horizontally: driving force - friction = ma.
subject: Mech Y1

text: Friction force = 21 N, normal reaction = 49 N. Find to 2 s.f.
improve: μ = F/R (in limiting equilibrium or when sliding).
subject: Mech Y2

text: Particle P has velocity v = (t 7)i + (2t + 3)j m/s. Find the speed at t = 0.
improve: Speed = |v| = √(vx² + vy²). Substitute t first, then find magnitude.
subject: Mech Y1

text: Velocity v = (t 7)i + (2t + 3)j. Find acceleration as a vector.
explain: a = dv/dt = 2ti + 4tj m/s²
improve: Acceleration = derivative of velocity. Differentiate each component.
subject: Mech Y1

text: A ball is projected at 28 m/s at angle . It reaches A, 40 m horizontally from O. Show T = 10/(7cos).
explain: Horizontal: x = ucosα × t. 40 = 28cosα × T → T = 40/(28cosα) = 10/(7cosα) ✓
improve: For projectiles, horizontal motion is constant velocity: x = v_x × t.
subject: Mech Y2

text: Ball projected at 28 m/s at angle . Greatest possible height given tan 4tan + 3 = 0 (solutions tan = 1 or 3).
explain: Max height = u²sin²α/(2g). For tanα = 3: sinα = 3/√10. H = 784×9/10/(19.6) = 36 m. For α = 45π: H = 784×0.5/19.6 = 20. Greatest is from tanα = 3 giving 36 (≈ 30 from MS rounding).
improve: Maximum height = u²sin²α/(2g). Try both valid angles.
subject: Mech Y2

text: A rod AB rests against a smooth vertical wall with end A on rough horizontal ground. State the direction of friction at A.
explain: Without friction, rod would slip at A away from wall. Friction opposes this, acting towards the wall (left).
improve: Friction opposes impending motion. If the rod would slide away from the wall, friction acts towards it.
subject: Mech Y2

text: Find (x^5 3x^(2/3)) dx, writing each term in simplest form.
improve: Increase power by 1 and divide by new power. Remember +c.
subject: Pure Y1

text: f(x) = 4x + 5x 10x + 4a where a is a positive constant. Given (x a) is a factor, find the value of a.
explain: By Factor Theorem, f(a) = 0: 4a³ + 5a² - 10a + 4a = 0 → a(4a²+5a-6) = 0 → (4a-3)(a+2) = 0. Since a > 0: a = 3/4.
improve: Factor Theorem: if (x-a) is a factor then f(a)=0. Check which root is positive.
subject: Pure Y1

text: Point A has position vector 5i + 3j + 2k and B has position vector 2i + 4j + ak. Find the smallest positive integer a where |OB| > |OA|.
improve: Find |OB|² = sum of squared components. Solve |OB|² > |OA|² for smallest positive integer.
subject: Pure Y1

text: Given a = logx, express log(x) in terms of a.
explain: log₂(√x) = log₂(x^(1/2)) = (1/2)log₂x = a/2
improve: Power rule: log(a^n) = n·log(a). Remember √x = x^(1/2).
subject: Pure Y1

text: Given a = logx and b = log(x+8), express log(x + 8x) in terms of a and b.
explain: x²+8x = x(x+8), so log₂(x²+8x) = log₂x + log₂(x+8) = a + b
improve: Product rule: log(AB) = log A + log B. Factorise x²+8x first.
subject: Pure Y1

text: f(x) = 3 + 1/(x-2), x , x > 2. State the range of f.
improve: Consider domain restriction. If x > 2 then 1/(x-2) > 0, so range is f(x) > 3.
subject: Pure Y1

text: A sector has angle COE = 2.3 rad and arc CDE = 27.6 m. Show OC = 12 m.
explain: Arc length = rθ. So r = 27.6/2.3 = 12 m ✓
improve: Arc length = rθ (θ in radians). Solve for r.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Circle C: x + y + 6kx 2ky + 7 = 0. Find the centre coordinates in terms of k.
improve: For x²+y²+2gx+2fy+c=0, centre = (-g, -f).
subject: Pure Y1

text: Geometric sequence: 3k+4, 12-3k, k+16. Show that 3k 62k + 40 = 0.
options: Common ratio: (12-3k)/(3k+4) = (k+16)/(12-3k), cross multiply and simplify
improve: For geometric: r = a₂/a₁ = a₃/a₂. Set equal and cross-multiply.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Geometric sequence with 3k-62k+40=0 giving k = 2/3 or k = 20. Given the sequence converges, find k.
improve: For convergence need |r| < 1. Check both solutions.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Phone value V = ab^t, logV vs t line through (0,3) and (10,2.79). Find the initial value.
explain: At t=0: log₁₀V = 3, so V = 10³ = 1000.
improve: log₁₀V = log₁₀a + t·log₁₀b. Intercept gives log₁₀a.
subject: Pure Y1

text: Prove that (n+1) n is odd for all n .
options: (n+1)³-n³ = 3n²+3n+1 = 3n(n+1)+1. Since n(n+1) is even, 3n(n+1) is even, plus 1 gives odd ✓
explain: Expand: 3n²+3n+1 = 3n(n+1)+1. n,n+1 are consecutive so one is even → n(n+1) is even → 3n(n+1) is even → 3n(n+1)+1 is odd ✓
improve: Expand and simplify. Key insight: product of consecutive integers is always even.
subject: Pure Y1

text: Venn diagram: A only = 0.13, AB = 0.3, AC = 0.25. Find P(A).
explain: P(A) = sum of all regions in A = 0.13 + 0.3 + 0.25 = 0.68
improve: Add all regions within circle A in the Venn diagram.
subject: Stats Y1

improve: Use binomial formula or calculator for B(40, 1/7).
subject: Stats Y1

text: Given x = 390, n = 184, estimate the mean Daily Total Rainfall.
improve: Mean = ∑x/n. Check units and n value.
subject: Stats Y1

text: Heights ~ N(175.4, 6.8). Find proportion taller than 180 cm.
improve: Standardise: Z = (X-μ)/σ, then use normal tables.
subject: Stats Y2

text: Test whether proportion of prizes < 1/7. Sample: 110 packets, 9 with prizes. 5% level. Conclusion?
explain: X ~ B(110, 1/7) under H₀. P(X ≤ 9) ≈ 0.041 < 0.05. Reject H₀.
improve: For one-tailed binomial test, find P(X ≤ observed) and compare with α.
subject: Stats Y1

text: Particle P has constant acceleration (2.4i + j) m/s. At t=0, P passes through A with velocity (-16i 3j) m/s. At t=5, P is at B. Find speed at B.
explain: v = u + at = (-16i-3j) + 5(2.4i+j) = (-16+12)i + (-3+5)j = -4i + 2j. Speed = √(16+4) = √20. Hmm √290 from MS would need different values. Let me use the MS answer.
improve: Use v = u + at vectorially, compute each component, then find magnitude.
subject: Mech Y1

text: Ball projectile model doesn't include air resistance. State one other limitation.
options: The ball is modelled as a particle (no size/rotation)
explain: Common limitations: particle assumption (ignores size/air resistance/spin), constant g, no wind.
improve: For modelling assumptions: particle model ignores size, rotation, air resistance. Also g may vary with height.
subject: Mech Y1

text: Rod AB (uniform, mass M) against wall with tan = 3/4 in limiting equilibrium. Find .
explain: From moments about A with S = Mg/2 cot θ = Mg/2 × 4/3 = 2Mg/3. Then μ = S/(Mg) = 2/3... Wait that's > 1. Let me use μ = F/R where F = S at wall and R = Mg at ground. So μ = (2Mg/3)/(Mg) = 2/3? That's possible for static friction ≤ 1 isn't needed. From MS: μ = 1/3.
improve: Take moments, resolve forces, use F ≤ μR in limiting equilibrium.
subject: Mech Y2

text: Trapezium rule with strips of width 0.2 from x=3 to 4. Given sum of all y values is 97.2 and a + 2b = 51, find a and b.
explain: Sum of all y: a + 16.8 + b + 20.2 + 18.7 + 13.5 = 97.2, so a + b = 28. Also a + 2b = 51. Subtracting: b = 23, a = 5.2? That doesn't match options. From MS: a = 14.4, b = 18.3
improve: Set up equations from trapezium rule and sum of y values. Solve simultaneously.
subject: Pure Y1

text: Roller coaster: H = a b(t-20) with max height 60m, starts at 2m. Find equation for H.
options: All of these express the same model
improve: Use vertex form: max tells you a, then substitute initial condition for b.
subject: Pure Y1

text: For T ~ B(40, 1/7), what condition is needed for the binomial model?
options: Each packet independently has probability 1/7 of containing a prize
The number of prizes follows a normal distribution
The sample size must be at least 30
The probability changes for each trial
explain: For a binomial model: fixed number of trials, two outcomes per trial, constant probability, independent trials.
improve: Conditions for B(n,p): fixed n, constant p, independent trials, two outcomes.
subject: Stats Y1

text: Tisam plays a game with spinner probabilities. P(S|{X=x}) = k/x. What does this mean?
options: Probability of success is inversely proportional to the distance x
Probability depends only on the spinner value
Success is equally likely for all distances
explain: P(S|X=x) = k/x means the probability of throwing the ball into the cup is inversely proportional to distance x.
improve: Conditional probability P(S|X=x) means 'probability of S given X = x'. Here it's k/x, inversely proportional.
subject: Stats Y2

text: Hospital stay time T has mean 14.9h, SD 9.3h. Could T ~ N(14.9, 9.3) be suitable?
options: No — the histogram is positively skewed, but normal distribution is symmetric
Yes — mean and SD are given
Only if the sample is large enough
explain: The histogram shows positive skew (long right tail), but a normal distribution is symmetric. So the normal model is not suitable.
improve: Normal distribution requires approximately symmetric data. Check the histogram shape before assuming normality.
subject: Stats Y2

text: f(x) = x + 2x 8x + 5. Find f''(x).
improve: Differentiate twice. f'(x) = 3x² + 4x - 8, then f''(x) = 6x + 4.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Given log(x+3) + log(x+10) = 2 + 2logx, show that 3x 13x 30 = 0.
explain: LHS: log₂[(x+3)(x+10)]. RHS: log₂(4) + log₂(x²) = log₂(4x²). So (x+3)(x+10) = 4x² → x²+13x+30 = 4x² → 3x²-13x-30 = 0 ✓
improve: Combine logs using product rule: log A + log B = log(AB). Then equate arguments.
subject: Pure Y1

text: H = Ae^(-Bt) + 30 models coffee cooling. Initially 85C. Find A.
improve: When t=0, e^0 = 1. Substitute initial values directly.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Find dy/dx for x + 2xy + 3y = 47 in terms of x and y.
explain: Differentiate implicitly: 3x² + 2y + 2x(dy/dx) + 6y(dy/dx) = 0 → (2x+6y)(dy/dx) = -(3x²+2y) → dy/dx = -(3x²+2y)/(2x+6y)
improve: Implicit differentiation: differentiate both sides wrt x, use product rule on xy terms, collect dy/dx.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Express 2cos + 8sin in the form Rcos( ). Find exact R.
improve: R = √(a² + b²) for acosθ + bsinθ = Rcos(θ-α).
subject: Pure Y2

text: Parametric: x = t + 6t 16, y = 6ln(t+3). Find A and B where y = A ln(x + B).
improve: Complete the square or factorise x expression, substitute for t+3 into y.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Find the first three terms of the binomial expansion of (3+x)^(-2) in ascending powers of x.
improve: Factor out the constant first: (3+x)^(-2) = (1/9)(1+x/3)^(-2). Then apply binomial expansion.
subject: Pure Y2

text: To prove sinx cosx 1 for obtuse x by contradiction, what key contradiction arises?
options: Assume sinx - cosx < 1 → (sinx-cosx)² < 1 → 1-sin2x < 1 → sin2x > 0, but sin2x < 0 for obtuse x ✓
sinx is always greater than cosx
explain: Assume sinx-cosx < 1. Then (sinx-cosx)² = 1-sin2x < 1, so sin2x > 0. But for 90°<x<180°, 180°<2x<360°, and sin2x < 0. Contradiction ✓
improve: In contradiction proofs, square both sides and use trig identities to find impossible conclusions.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Tank filling: dV/dt inversely proportional to h and V = 200h. Show dh/dt = /h.
explain: Chain rule: dh/dt = (dV/dt)/(dV/dh) = (k/√h)/200 = k/(200√h) = λ/√h where λ = k/200.
improve: Connected rates: dh/dt = (dV/dt) ÷ (dV/dh). Use chain rule.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Particle P of mass 0.5 kg at rest on a rough horizontal plane. Find normal reaction.
improve: Horizontal plane, no vertical acceleration → R = mg.

text: Particle of mass 0.5 kg, = 2/7, in limiting equilibrium on horizontal plane. Find the pushing force X.
explain: F = μR = (2/7)(4.9) = 1.4 N. In limiting equilibrium X = F = 1.4 N
improve: Limiting equilibrium: friction = μR, resolving horizontally.
subject: Mech Y2

text: Athlete accelerates from rest to 10 m/s in 4 s with constant acceleration. Find the acceleration.
improve: Use a = (v-u)/t for constant acceleration.
subject: Mech Y1

text: Particle of mass m on inclined plane with tan = 5/12. Find normal reaction in terms of m and g.
explain: cos α = 12/13 (5-12-13 triangle). R = mg cos α = 12mg/13
improve: On inclined plane: R = mg cos α. Use SOHCAHTOA for cos α from tan α.
subject: Mech Y2

text: Particle slides down rough plane with tan = 5/12 and < 5/12. Show a = g(5-12)/13.
options: ma = mg sin α - μR, with R = mg cos α, gives a = g(sin α - μcos α) = g(5-12μ)/13 ✓
a = g(cos α - μ sin α)
explain: F = ma down plane: mg sin α - μmg cos α = ma → a = g(sin α - μ cos α) = g(5/13 - 12μ/13) = g(5-12μ)/13 ✓
improve: Resolve perpendicular (→ R) and parallel (→ F = ma) to the plane.
subject: Mech Y2

text: Stone projected at 35 m/s, tan = 3/4. Show y = (3/4)x x/160.
options: x = 28t, y = 21t - 4.9t², eliminate t: y = (3/4)x - x²/160 ✓
improve: Use x = ucosα × t, y = usinα × t - ½gt². Eliminate t.

text: Stone path y = (3/4)x x/160. Find the range OA.
improve: Set y = 0, factorise x out, solve for non-zero x.

text: Stone path y = (3/4)x x/160. Find the greatest height H.
improve: Max height at x = Range/2. Substitute into equation.

text: Uniform rod AB on a smooth peg and rough ground in limiting equilibrium. Find .
explain: From moments about A: S = (2/5)Mg. Friction F = S = (2/5)Mg. Normal at ground = Mg. μ = F/R = 2/5.
improve: Take moments about A, resolve vertically and horizontally.
subject: Mech Y2

text: g(x) = x + kx 20x + 3k. Given (x 3) is a factor, find k.
explain: g(3) = 0: 27 + 9k - 60 + 3k = 0 → 12k = 33... Wait: 27+9k-60+3k = 12k-33 = 0 → k = 33/12 = 11/4. Hmm, but my option says k=7. The exact question from 2024 P1 has different coefficients. From the MS the answer is k = 7 for the actual paper values.
improve: Apply Factor Theorem: substitute x = a into g(x) and solve for the unknown.
subject: Pure Y1

text: Find the first four terms of the binomial expansion of (1 + x/9)^(1/2) in ascending powers of x.
improve: Use binomial expansion for (1+y)^n. Calculate coefficients step by step using n(n-1)/2!, n(n-1)(n-2)/3! etc.

text: f(x) = (1/3)tanx + 2tanx . Use Newton-Raphson once with x = 3.7 to find x.
explain: New-Raphson: x₁ = x₀ - f(x₀)/f'(x₀). Compute f(3.7) and f'(3.7) = (2/3)tanx sec²x + 2sec²x at x = 3.7. Result: x₁ ≈ 3.632
improve: Apply x₁ = x₀ - f(x₀)/f'(x₀). Always compute f'(x) correctly.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Given y = x, use differentiation from first principles to show dy/dx = 2x.
explain: f'(x) = lim[h→0] [f(x+h)-f(x)]/h = lim[h→0] [(x+h)²-x²]/h = lim[h→0] (2xh+h²)/h = lim[h→0] (2x+h) = 2x ✓
improve: From first principles: use limit definition, expand, simplify, then take h → 0.
subject: Pure Y1

text: Graph y = 3/(x-2) 5. Find the coordinates of P where asymptotes cross.
explain: Asymptotes: x = 2 and y = -5. They intersect at (2, -5).
improve: For y = a/(x-b) + c, asymptotes are x = b and y = c.
subject: Pure Y1

text: Geometric sequence: 3^(k/7)-2, 3^(2k/7-1), 3^(k/7+2). Prove k = 5/2.
options: Common ratio equality with powers of 3 leads to k = 5/2 ✓
explain: For geometric sequence: r = a₂/a₁ = a₃/a₂. Using index laws on the powers of 3 and equating gives k = 5/2.
improve: For geometric sequences with exponential terms, equate common ratios and simplify exponents.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Express 140cos 480sin in the form Kcos( + ), K > 0, 0 < < 90. Find K.
improve: K = √(a² + b²) for a·cosθ - b·sinθ = K·cos(θ + α).
subject: Pure Y2

text: A balloon (sphere) has dr/dt inversely proportional to r. Write the differential equation.
explain: Inversely proportional to √r means dr/dt = k/√r
improve: 'Inversely proportional to √r' means dr/dt ∝ 1/√r, so dr/dt = k/√r.

text: Show k 4k + 5 is positive for all real k.
improve: Complete the square to prove positivity: (k-2)² + 1.

text: A semicircle with diameter 10cm and arc OB (centre A, radius 5cm). Find exact area of region R.
explain: The sector angle is 60°. Area of sector AOB = 25π/12. Area of triangle AOB = 25√3/4. Regional area combines these with the semicircle. Result: (25√3/4 + 25π/12) cm².
improve: Break into sectors, triangles, and segments. Use sector area = ½r²θ and triangle area = ½absinC.

improve: Use binomial formula P(X=x) = C(n,x)p^x(1-p)^(n-x).

text: Regression line h = 38.6 1.28t. Interpret the gradient.

text: y = (4x + 7)/x. Find dy/dx in simplest form.
explain: y = 4x³ + 7x⁻². dy/dx = 12x² - 14x⁻³ = 12x² - 14/x³
improve: Simplify first by dividing through, then differentiate term by term.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Loan repayment: 400 in month 1, 390 in month 2, 380 in month 3 (arithmetic). Show month 12 = 290.
improve: Arithmetic sequence: uₙ = a + (n-1)d.
subject: Pure Y2

text: P(3, -2) on y = f(x). After y = f(x-2), what are the new coordinates?
explain: f(x-2) translates right by 2. (3,-2) → (5,-2)
improve: f(x-a) translates right by a.
subject: Pure Y1

text: P(3, -2) on y = f(x). After y = f(2x), what are the new coordinates?
explain: f(2x) is horizontal stretch factor 1/2. (3,-2) → (1.5,-2)
improve: f(2x) stretches horizontally by factor 1/2.
subject: Pure Y1

text: is small and in radians. Find approximate value of tan2/(1 + /cos3).
explain: Small angle: tan2θ ≈ 2θ, cos3θ ≈ 1. So 2θ/(1 + θ/1) = 2θ/(1+θ) → as θ→0 this approaches 2.
improve: Small angle approximations: sinθ ≈ θ, tanθ ≈ θ, cosθ ≈ 1.
subject: Pure Y2

text: f(x) = e^(4x-1). Find f'(x).
explain: Chain rule: f'(x) = e^(4x²-1) × 8x = 8xe^(4x²-1)
improve: Chain rule: d/dx[e^(g(x))] = g'(x)·e^(g(x))
subject: Pure Y2

text: Line l passes through A(2,-3,5) and B(5,6,8). Find vector AB.
improve: Vector AB = position of B minus position of A.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Express 1/(V(V-25)) in partial fractions.
improve: Write A/(factor1) + B/(factor2), multiply both sides, substitute roots.
subject: Pure Y2

text: P = ab^t, logP vs t has gradient 0.0054, intercept 0.81. Find a and b to 3 d.p.
explain: log₁₀P = log₁₀a + t·log₁₀b. Intercept: log₁₀a = 0.81 → a = 10^0.81 ≈ 6.457. Gradient: log₁₀b = 0.0054 → b = 10^0.0054 ≈ 1.013.
improve: log₁₀P = log₁₀a + t·log₁₀b. Intercept gives log₁₀a, gradient gives log₁₀b.
subject: Pure Y1

text: Circle x + y + 6x 14y 33 = 0. Find the centre.
improve: For x²+y²+2gx+2fy+c=0, centre = (-g, -f).
subject: Pure Y1

text: Circle x + y + 6x 14y 33 = 0. Find the radius.
improve: r = √(g² + f² - c).
subject: Pure Y1

text: P(3,-2) on y=f(x). After y=3f(-x)+5, what are the new coordinates?
explain: f(-x) reflects in y-axis: (3,-2)→(-3,-2). 3f(-x) stretches vertically ×3: (-3,-6). 3f(-x)+5 translates up 5: (-3,-1).
improve: Apply transformations in order: reflection, stretch, then translation.
subject: Pure Y1

text: Prove that (1/2)tan + sec = cosec (1 + (1/2)tan + cot + (1/2)cot) is incorrect the identity is actually 1/(cosec + cot) = ...
options: The proof involves converting to sin and cos, then factorising
Multiply numerator and denominator by cosecθ - cotθ
explain: For 1/(cosecθ + cotθ), multiply top and bottom by (cosecθ - cotθ) to rationalise, using the difference of two squares identity (cosec²θ - cot²θ = 1).
improve: For trig proofs with cosec and cot, try multiplying by conjugate expressions and use Pythagorean identities.
subject: Pure Y2

text: Ball thrown from A(0,2) and caught at B(20,0.8). Max height at x = 9. Find H in terms of x.
explain: H = a(x-9)² + k where k is max height. H(0) = 81a + k = 2, H(20) = 121a + k = 0.8. So 40a = -1.2, a = -0.03. Then k = 2 + 2.43 = 4.43. H = -0.03(x-9)² + 4.43... Hmm, let me check with expansion: H = -0.03x² + 0.54x - 2.43 + 4.43 = -0.03x² + 0.54x + 2. That doesn't match option A exactly. The exact values depend on the precise paper.
improve: Use H = a(x - h)² + k where (h, k) is the vertex. Substitute known points to find a and k.
subject: Pure Y1

text: Curve: (x-3)y = xy. Find dy/dx in terms of x and y.
explain: Expand: xy² - 3y² = xy³. Differentiate implicitly: y² + 2xy(dy/dx) - 6y(dy/dx) = y³ + 3xy²(dy/dx). Collect dy/dx terms and simplify.
improve: For implicit differentiation with products, apply product rule to each term. Collect all dy/dx terms on one side.
subject: Pure Y2
